issue_title: Promql: weird FORK error message on simple query
issue_body:
Reproducer:

Run local instance with all of csv spec test data:
```
./gradlew run

./gradlew :x-pack:plugin:esql:qa:testFixtures:loadCsvSpecData --args="http://elastic-admin:elastic-password@localhost:9200"            
```

Now, run this simple PROMQL query:
```
url -u elastic:password -H "Content-Type: application/json" "127.0.0.1:9200/_query?format=txt" -d '
{                            
  "query": "PROMQL avg(cpu)"}
'

{"error":{"root_cause":[{"type":"illegal_argument_exception","reason":"FORK supports up to 8 branches, got: 9"}],"type":"illegal_argument_exception","reason":"FORK supports up to 8 branches, got: 9"},"status":400}
```

Don't know if this is related to PROMQL or has anything to do with views resolution (otherwise I cannot understand where the FORK error message comes from).

@craigtaverner , could you maybe have a look?

commit_message:
[Transform] Only run next search once (#148268)

If we fail to call `next.run()` within the `doSaveState` function, then
we should not immediately try to call `next.run()` again. This can
happen during a shutdown scenario, where `next.run()` will try to use a
threadpool that is shutting down, and the indexer should exit out
anyway.

Resolve #147716

commit_message:
Stabilize fetch chunk tests: breaker busy-wait, avoid acquireResponseStream poll leak (#148026)

Fixes flaky tests and a refcount leak in TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests by waiting on REQUEST circuit breaker usage instead of retrying acquireResponseStream inside assertBusy + expectThrows. Aligns TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests with async processChunk cleanup.

Problem
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: processChunk can still run on TRANSPORT_WORKER after the test releases refs; an immediate breaker.getUsed() == 0 can race tearDown and ESTestCase#allBreakersMemoryReleased.
TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests: assertBusy(() -> expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> acquireResponseStream(...))) leaks refs when the stream is still registered (acquire succeeds, expectThrows fails with AssertionError, assertBusy retries without decRef).
Solution
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationWhenDataNodeFailsAfterChunkStreaming: replace the busy loop with assertBusy until REQUEST breaker getUsed() == 0 only (no following expectThrows + acquireResponseStream).
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationOnLastChunkDeserializationFailure: same breaker assertBusy, then a single expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> activeFetchPhaseTasks.acquireResponseStream(...)) to assert deregistration without a retry loop around acquire.
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: wrap the final breaker release check in assertBusy.

Closes #147812
Closes #147974
Closes #147870
Closes #147967

Unmutes tests.

commit_message:
Fix warming logic related to skipping region 0 (#148155)

The warming code skips warming a range if it is found to
be replicated and the replicated range is in region 0. There
were two errors here:

1. Region 0 isn't guaranteed to be fully cached when warming
runs, at least on search search shards. When search shards
open, they read headers from the start of the file, which
can trigger a cache fill. But search shards can fill the
cache by reading commits from their corresponding index
shards, and when they do they carefully only read to the
page-aligned end of headers rather than fetching a full
cache region as they would from the object store.

2. If a replicated range starts in region 0 but spans
regions, then the warming code would skip the front
part of the range, and then warm the back part from the
actual file rather than the replicated range. That part
isn't necessarily contiguous, so when Lucene tries to read
the replicated range and does do a contiguous read, it may
generate a cache miss on the back part of the range.

Problem 1 is addressed by disabling the region 0 skip
for search shards.
Problem 2 is fixed by moving the replicated range translation
before region calculation in the warming code.

Fixes #147843

issue_title: ES|QL: snapshot conversion functions can be used in release builds
issue_body:
To be clear, this only concerns when we use the `::` shortcut.

For example, in a release build, the following will fail as expected:

```
ROW str = "2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z..2021-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
| EVAL range = to_date_range(str)
```

with:

```
Unknown function [to_date_range], did you mean any of [to_date_nanos, to_datenanos, to_datetime, date_parse, to_dateperiod, to_string, to_cartesianshape]?
```

However, if we use the `::date_range` notation instead, we will not get the same error:

```
ROW str = "2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z..2021-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
| EVAL range = str::date_range
```

Instead we get results back and column with type that should only be available under snapshot:
```
                       str                        |                      range                       
--------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------
2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z..2021-01-01T00:00:00.000Z|2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z..2021-01-01T00:00:00.000Z
```

It looks like `EsqlDataTypeConverter` does not take into account which functions are available only under snapshot.

commit_message:
Stabilize fetch chunk tests: breaker busy-wait, avoid acquireResponseStream poll leak (#148026)

Fixes flaky tests and a refcount leak in TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests by waiting on REQUEST circuit breaker usage instead of retrying acquireResponseStream inside assertBusy + expectThrows. Aligns TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests with async processChunk cleanup.

Problem
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: processChunk can still run on TRANSPORT_WORKER after the test releases refs; an immediate breaker.getUsed() == 0 can race tearDown and ESTestCase#allBreakersMemoryReleased.
TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests: assertBusy(() -> expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> acquireResponseStream(...))) leaks refs when the stream is still registered (acquire succeeds, expectThrows fails with AssertionError, assertBusy retries without decRef).
Solution
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationWhenDataNodeFailsAfterChunkStreaming: replace the busy loop with assertBusy until REQUEST breaker getUsed() == 0 only (no following expectThrows + acquireResponseStream).
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationOnLastChunkDeserializationFailure: same breaker assertBusy, then a single expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> activeFetchPhaseTasks.acquireResponseStream(...)) to assert deregistration without a retry loop around acquire.
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: wrap the final breaker release check in assertBusy.

Closes #147812
Closes #147974
Closes #147870
Closes #147967

Unmutes tests.

commit_message:
[Test] Fix StatelessMergeIT.testOnlyForceMergesAreAllowedDuringRelocation (#148035)

The test failed on CI because the `resumeHandoff` count down latch
wasn't completed in time. Indeed, safeAwait() started to wait for the
force merge to complete, something that didn't happen in the 20 seconds
time window:

```
[2026-04-29T09:05:47,063] --> start relocation paused
[2026-04-29T09:06:05,388] --> force merge call
[2026-04-29T09:06:07] java.lang.AssertionError: safeAwait: CountDownLatch did not reach zero within the timeout
      at org.elasticsearch.xpack.stateless.StatelessMergeIT$1.await(StatelessMergeIT.java:141)
```

Fixed by extending the timeout to 1 minute since I'm less comfortable
in changing the number of triggered merges/refreshes.

Closes #147880

commit_message:
Tweak testThrottlingMetrics config for stability (#148762)

Tweak the configuration of SnapshotMetricsIT testThrottlingMetrics to make it more stable and less likely to timeout of a CI machine.

The problem was that the test created too many chunks causing uploads take too long cumulatively. This caused snapshot creation to take over 10s resulting is timeout of safeGet. For that we increase the chunk size. Additionally, we also saw timeouts during restore caused by slow diagnostic checks. For that we decrease the number of shards and docs. Neither change should affect test coverage.

Closes #147954

commit_message:
[Test] Fix CreateIndexLimitIT.testCreateIndexLimit (#148196)

The test was hardcoding ?version=master in the expected URL, but
in release tests that are run with -Dbuild.snapshot=false, the version
becomes 9.5 instead of master.

This PR fixes the test by asserting using the constant ReferenceDocs
.MAX_INDICES_PER_PROJECT directly, which generates the correct URL for
snapshot and non-snapshot builds.

Closes #147956

commit_message:
Stabilize fetch chunk tests: breaker busy-wait, avoid acquireResponseStream poll leak (#148026)

Fixes flaky tests and a refcount leak in TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests by waiting on REQUEST circuit breaker usage instead of retrying acquireResponseStream inside assertBusy + expectThrows. Aligns TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests with async processChunk cleanup.

Problem
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: processChunk can still run on TRANSPORT_WORKER after the test releases refs; an immediate breaker.getUsed() == 0 can race tearDown and ESTestCase#allBreakersMemoryReleased.
TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests: assertBusy(() -> expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> acquireResponseStream(...))) leaks refs when the stream is still registered (acquire succeeds, expectThrows fails with AssertionError, assertBusy retries without decRef).
Solution
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationWhenDataNodeFailsAfterChunkStreaming: replace the busy loop with assertBusy until REQUEST breaker getUsed() == 0 only (no following expectThrows + acquireResponseStream).
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationOnLastChunkDeserializationFailure: same breaker assertBusy, then a single expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> activeFetchPhaseTasks.acquireResponseStream(...)) to assert deregistration without a retry loop around acquire.
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: wrap the final breaker release check in assertBusy.

Closes #147812
Closes #147974
Closes #147870
Closes #147967

Unmutes tests.
